title: China-to-Vietnam Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Re-Validation vs China GB/T 37408 / GB/T 19964 Baseline
rows.target_requirement: Vietnam uses a 50 Hz electricity system, but a China 50 Hz inverter configuration should still be re-validated against the Vietnam project grid-code settings, voltage level, protection windows, reconnection timing, ride-through settings, harmonic limits, and EVN commissioning requirements. Passing the China baseline does not automatically prove the final Vietnam firmware profile is acceptable.
target_standards: Vietnam MOIT electricity-system rules for the applicable voltage level
EVN project commissioning and interconnection requirements
rows.china_equivalent: The common China baseline includes GB/T 37408-2019 for grid-connected PV inverter technical requirements and GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power-station access to the power system. These documents are a strong starting point for engineering comparison but are not Vietnam grid approval documents.
rows.gap: Prepare a Vietnam parameter file rather than shipping a generic China setting. Evidence should tie the hardware, firmware, country code, anti-islanding logic, protection thresholds, and commissioning test records to the actual Vietnam site or project class.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as part of grid-connection acceptance where EVN or the project reviewer requires Vietnam-specific settings and commissioning evidence.
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T baseline evidence is not transferable without Vietnam parameter review and project acceptance.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A 50 Hz China profile is only a baseline. Re-validate the inverter with Vietnam project settings before grid approval or commissioning.

rows.requirement: EMC and Radio-Module Scope for Connected Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Vietnam EMC obligations for a PV inverter depend on the exact product scope and any communications functions. A base power-conversion unit, a gateway-equipped inverter, and an inverter with embedded Wi-Fi, cellular, Bluetooth, or other radio modules may fall into different technical-regulation, conformity, labeling, or radio approval paths. Confirm the current Vietnam QCVN / MIC / project scope before relying on China EMC or radio reports.
target_standards: Applicable Vietnam QCVN EMC technical regulations by product scope
Vietnam MIC radio equipment rules where intentional transmitters are included
Project EMC and power-quality specifications where imposed by owner or utility
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin packages may include GB/T 17626 immunity reports, GB 17625 harmonic or flicker reports, SRRC approvals for radio modules, and CE EMC reports. These documents can support engineering review but do not automatically satisfy Vietnam QCVN, MIC, label, or project-specific acceptance requirements.
rows.gap: Classify each SKU separately. The gap is often in the communications variant, external gateway, antenna, firmware, label, user notice, and local representative evidence rather than the power stage alone. Prepare a Vietnam scope memo before import or project submission.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where the inverter, gateway, or radio module is in scope of Vietnam technical regulations, MIC radio rules, labeling rules, or project-specific EMC requirements.
mandatory_status.cn: Not transferable — Chinese EMC, SRRC, or CE EMC evidence does not automatically satisfy Vietnam conformity or project requirements.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat EMC and radio-module compliance as a separate Vietnam scope check for each inverter SKU and communications configuration.

source.publisher: Ministry of Information and Communications of Viet Nam (MIC)

rows.requirement: MOIT and EVN Grid Connection Code and Anti-Islanding Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied PV inverter projects in Vietnam must be reviewed against the applicable MOIT electricity-system rules and the EVN interconnection route for the actual voltage level and project type. Anti-islanding evidence may be requested through IEC 62116, IEC 61727, or project-specific technical specifications, but the enforceable obligation comes from the applicable grid-connection approval and electricity-system rules rather than from the IEC standards by themselves.
target_standards: MOIT electricity transmission and distribution system regulations
EVN interconnection procedures for grid-connected generation
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese documentation often includes GB/T 37408-2019 inverter technical requirements, GB/T 19964-2024 PV power-station grid connection requirements, and IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 reports. These are useful baseline documents but must be re-mapped to Vietnam's connection study, relay settings, voltage/frequency windows, and EVN acceptance process.
rows.gap: The practical gap is the project-specific interconnection package. Exporters should prepare inverter type-test reports, anti-islanding test evidence, grid-code parameter tables, protection settings, firmware version controls, harmonic and power-quality data, and site commissioning records in a form EVN or the project reviewer can trace to the actual model and settings.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection where the inverter is part of a grid-connected project; IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 evidence is mandatory only where required by the applicable approval route, technical specification, or contract.
mandatory_status.cn: China grid-code evidence is project-specific and not directly transferable to Vietnam interconnection approval.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat anti-islanding and grid-code evidence as a Vietnam project approval workstream, not as a generic IEC report handover.

source.publisher: Ministry of Industry and Trade of Viet Nam (MOIT)

rows.requirement: Rooftop Solar, DPPA, and Self-Consumption Rules
rows.target_requirement: Vietnam's current rooftop-solar compliance route depends on the project model. Decree 80/2024/ND-CP created a direct power purchase mechanism for renewable-energy generators and large electricity users, while Decree 135/2024/ND-CP addresses mechanisms and policies for self-produced, self-consumed rooftop solar power. EVN's 2026 public guidance continues to frame rooftop solar as self-produced, self-consumed project development and points users to current Government and MOIT procedures. The inverter file should therefore be matched to whether the installation is behind-the-meter self-consumption, excess-power arrangement, DPPA-related generation, or another grid-connected project route.
target_standards: Applicable MOIT / EVN implementation procedures for the project model
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin documentation may describe Chinese distributed PV project filings, self-consumption/export settings, grid-dispatch settings, and Chinese owner acceptance records. These documents can explain the product configuration but do not establish eligibility under Vietnam's rooftop-solar, DPPA, or EVN interconnection route.
china_standards: China distributed PV project filing documents
rows.gap: The commercial model changes the compliance package. For Vietnam, confirm whether the inverter needs export-limitation controls, metering interfaces, remote dispatch or telemetry, project registration documents, cybersecurity or communications evidence, and local owner declarations tied to the specific rooftop or DPPA route. The proposed Decision 1570/QD-EVN (2024) SCADA/monitoring requirement for systems >=100 kW could not be verified from official sources, so do not treat that exact decision number or threshold as confirmed without fresh EVN confirmation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory at project level where the installation seeks Vietnam rooftop-solar, self-consumption, DPPA, electricity sale, or grid-connection treatment. Not a blanket whole-unit inverter certification requirement by itself.
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese distributed-generation approvals and settings are not substitutes for Vietnam project eligibility or EVN/MOIT procedures.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Classify the Vietnam project route before finalizing inverter settings and documents; rooftop self-consumption and DPPA files are not interchangeable, and the proposed Decision 1570/QD-EVN >=100 kW SCADA threshold remains unverified.

rows.requirement: TCVN Adoption of IEC 62109-1/-2 Inverter Safety
rows.target_requirement: Vietnamese project files for PV inverters may request safety evidence aligned to TCVN 12231-1:2018 and TCVN 12231-2:2018, the Vietnamese adoptions corresponding to IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 for power converters used in photovoltaic power systems. Treat the TCVN / IEC evidence as a technical safety dossier item unless a specific tender, grid-connection approval, import program, or legal instrument makes it mandatory for the transaction. A proposed mandatory energy-efficiency-labeling claim for PV inverters under MOIT Circular 12/2026/TT-BCT and TCVN 8929:2026 effective 1-Jun-2026 could not be verified from official sources, so it is not treated here as a confirmed requirement.
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin inverter files commonly include IEC CB reports to IEC 62109-1/-2, CQC or CGC voluntary certificates, and China project safety evidence. These can support Vietnam review, but the Vietnam-facing package should map the report editions and national deviations to the current TCVN / project requirement.
china_standards: CQC / CGC voluntary PV inverter certification
rows.gap: The gap is not only whether IEC 62109 testing exists, but whether the Vietnamese buyer, utility reviewer, or project owner accepts the edition, certificate holder, factory address, model family, and Vietnamese documentation crosswalk. Prepare a TCVN-to-IEC clause matrix, model list, critical component list, labels, manuals, and any Vietnamese-language declarations requested by the project.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-required unless a specific Vietnam legal instrument, tender, or interconnection approval makes the TCVN / IEC 62109 evidence mandatory for the product or project. The proposed Circular 12/2026/TT-BCT + TCVN 8929:2026 inverter-labeling mandate was not verified.
mandatory_status.cn: Usually voluntary or project-driven for export files; China IEC/CQC/CGC evidence does not automatically become Vietnam approval.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not assume an IEC 62109 CB report alone closes the Vietnam safety file, and do not rely on the unverified Circular 12/2026/TT-BCT + TCVN 8929:2026 labeling claim without official confirmation. Crosswalk evidence to the current TCVN / project requirement and confirm acceptance with the buyer or approving party.

source.publisher: Vietnam Standards and Quality Institute (VSQI) / STAMEQ
